title: Tools dispatch: replace 50-arm match in execute_tool_with_memory with ToolHandler registry (OCP/SRP)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Architectural Alignment / Structural Modularity (OCP violation)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/mod.rs lines 807-856 (execute_tool_with_memory), 876-892 (execute_tool_full), 1023-1086 (execute_tool_with_tasks)

**Violation:**
The project hosts THREE overlapping string-keyed dispatch switches that must be edited in lockstep whenever a new tool is added: execute_tool_with_memory (~50 arms), execute_tool_full (task/agent_output short-circuit then delegation), and execute_tool_with_tasks (task_* short-circuit then delegation). This is a classic Open/Closed Principle violation — the dispatch table is closed for extension without modification in three places, and new tools must thread through all three layers (see execute_tool_with_memory's final arm at line 849-853 where task/agent_output returns an explicit 'configuration context required' error, meaning the low-level dispatcher is aware of the high-level dispatcher's responsibilities — a Big Ball of Mud coupling). The function is also 1920 lines total; this single file mixes tool schema (JSON), dispatch, permission checking, marker parsing, and ~800 lines of tests.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Define a trait:
   pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
       fn name(&self) -> &'static str;
       fn definition(&self) -> serde_json::Value;
       fn execute(&self, args: &HashMap<String, Value>, ctx: &ToolContext) -> (String, bool);
   }
   where ToolContext bundles optional MemoryDb, AppConfig, TaskManager (replacing the current 3-function overload set with a single dispatch function parameterised by context).
2. Each tool module (bash/, file/, web.rs, chainlink.rs, cron.rs, worktree.rs, etc.) registers its handler(s) through an inventory-style collect (e.g. linkme or manual register_tools() fn).
3. Reduce execute_tool_* to: registry.get(name).ok_or(UnknownTool).and_then(|h| h.execute(&args, &ctx)).
4. Move the JSON definitions currently hard-coded in get_tool_definitions() (lines 104-788) onto each handler's definition() method so schema and behaviour live together (SRP).
5. Split mod.rs into: dispatch.rs (ToolHandler + registry), schema.rs (OpenAI-function-format helpers), markers.rs (USER_QUESTION_MARKER, parse_user_questions, parse_exit_plan_mode_prompts, check_tool_result_marker), permissions.rs (check_tool_permission), and move the ~800 lines of tests to per-tool files.
